We found this pretty female murderbird along a roadside near Nanegalito, Ecuador. American Kestrels have a large range, from most of North American south into the western portion of South America and then throughout most of the southern portion of the continent.
🪶🌿🧪

Wonky winter wildlife! For some reason this Gray Catbird decided to stay on the north shore of Lake Ontario this winter. It was chowing down on invasive European Buckthorn berries which are aplenty so I guess it's doing well enough. 🪶

Endangered California gnatcatcher in the wonderful coastal sage habitat here down by the Mexican border in the Tijuana River Valley open space preserve. In the first shot he is doing his mewing call that sounds like a kitten. 🪶

Broad-billed motmot with a quite water-soaked feather dress.

Bet he was as surprised as me when the sudden rain shower started 🌧️

Spotting it was pure luck: Motmots usually wait motionless on branches until potential prey moves (insects).

🪶
#birds
#costarica

A great part of Sayulita, Mexico: there's one patch of the Pacific Ocean that apparently is filled with the dumbest fish. Over and over brown pelicans speared into the water, shooting out their heads at the last moment like a snake striking to snatch fish from the water. #Mexico #Birds #Birding

ライチョウの雄、雪の中から一生懸命に掘り出した松ぼっくりを転がして立ち去る。すると雌が食べにくる。優しすぎる #birds

A male Ptarmigan dug a pine cone out of the snow but didn't eat it. Then the female comes to eat. So gentle I'm impressed
Ran into this 2-year-old bald eagle on a smoky Sunday evening. It takes about 5 years for these long-lived birds to develop their definitive plumage. #birds
This is the Cameroon two-horned mountain chameleon. It's endangered but more importantly it's cute as heck.

Since you’re enjoying the red-winged blackbird pic (& name), here are some tricolored blackbirds. Diff species. Found mostly in CA. Grassland specialists. Endangered.

Similar to red-winged, but males have white wing patch (not orange/yellow) and females are darker with clearer eyebrow stripe.

#BirdGlamour’s Halloween-colored birds continues with Black-vented Orioles! These blackbird relatives call Central & South America home but are occasionally spotted in AZ & TX. Black-vented Orioles love an open area with scattered trees. Their name comes from their black vent (aka bum) area. 🐡🧪

The kingfisher that lives by my place finally agreed to let me photograph her! Just after this she caught a fish nearly a third her size and proceeded to whack it against the branch until it was dead.

Belted Kingfisher - San Francisco, CA 🪶
Doesn’t get much more magnificent than Trumpeter swans in Oregon’s desert country. These amazing birds against these rugged landscapes are just beautiful.

Many think of Marbled Godwits as prairie birds (well coastal in winter) but there are also breeding populations on the James Bay lowlands and on the Alaska Peninsula. This one is from the #Alaska population during a stopover on the Anchor River State Recreation Area 🪶

Bird Glamour’s Glamager series continues with Hepatic Tanagers! This southern US to Central American dark red bird loves a pine-oak forest. These birds are little studied, but we do know they eat insects, spiders, and small fruits. Lady Hepatic Tanagers do most of the nest building. 🐡

Welcome to the #BirdGlamour Glamager series, starting with the Western Tanager from western North America! The Western Tanager is extra and DOES NOT get his red coloration from a diet rich in carotenoids like many birds do: their red comes from rhodoxanthin from their insect-rich diet. 🐡🐦

I learned on Saturday that California has its own race of Red-winged Blackbird that has no yellow in its shoulder patch - the California Bicolored Blackbird.

I gotta admit: these birds were stunners. It’s amazing what a little color difference can do.

god i love watching a reddish egret just launch itself through the water, wings out, head swinging to and fro. It's a hilarious method of hunting AND IT WORKS. these dudes rustle up a ton of food running around like a maniac in the water.

Great egrets: masters of the art of striking a pose. With a slow, sassy strut, they wade through the waters in pursuit of their aquatic prey. Showcasing their prowess as ambush predators, these birds are striking in more than one way! Nature’s choreography unfolds in these feathered maestros.

Best bird photos from 2023.
When word got out that rare scarlet-chested parrots had been seen in big numbers at Gluepot, birders (including me) flocked to see them. I had searched for these birds for years. This is a female scarlet-chested parrot feeding on cottony saltbush.
🪶 #birds

Bird of the day.. Waxwing (Bombycilla garrulus) We’ve had quite the irruption of these bandit masked bounty hunters this year! Our locale is blessed with juniper, rowan & mistletoe berries. They now move on to orchards new.. a joy all told these past few weeks..🪶

A Montane Woodcreeper working on a tasty moth. The bird is bashing the moth on the tree to knock off the scales on the moth's wings. These birds hitch up trees like woodpeckers while foraging for insects. 🪶🌿🧪
Bellavista Cloud Forest Reserve, Ecuador

A stunning Scarlet-Bellied Mountain Tanager working on some feather care - the bird was happily bathing. These fruit eating birds can be found in the high forests of the Andes Mountains from Venezuela to Bolivia. 🌿🧪🪶🐡
Zuroloma Birding, Nono, Ecuador
A great blue heron about to devour a crayfish.  Taken just a few days ago, 3-Oct-2023 in Sterling, Virginia, USA.

After years (decades) of waiting, I finally got to see some Waxwings today, as a small flock visited Brandon Marsh near Coventry at the same time I did. These birds are absolute rock stars and I am beyond thrilled to have seen them at last! 😍#BirdPhotography #NaturePhotography #WildlifePhotography 🌿

I hear it's Black-crowned night heron day. Count me in. They're such sleek birds. Found on every continent except Australia and Antarctica, they are the most widespread heron species in the world! #Birds

When birding in your own garden in South Africa gets interesting. A pair of African Hoopoes successfully raised a Greater Honeyguide. 
#Southern African birds #birding

This morning on the Boston side of the river, by Mass Ave and Storrow, I saw a young female bald eagle swoop into the Charles and then a second later it was flying over me with a fish in its talons. I have trouble with earnestness, but seeing bald eagles in the middle of the city is so fucking cool.
Various waterbirds, taken from a great distance during our yurt stay last week: red-necked grebe, common loon, red-breasted mergansers. All perfectly at home on the aptly-named Bird Lake in Manitoba.

Gray Catbird numbers are way down from last week. Most of the pokeweed, Virginia creeper, and wild grape vines have been pretty well stripped of their fruits. I suspect this may be one of the last ones I see until next spring. Safe travels! #birds #fall #migration 🪶

It's winter (weird duck season) in the Northern hemisphere, so here are two of the weird Australian ducks 🪶 from this area. Plumed whistling ducks - they whistle but don't quack - and pink-eared ducks, aka "zebra shovellers"
Brisbane, QLD
##ausbirds

Nice mixed flock of mostly White-eyed Parakeets (Psittacara leucophthalmus) with a couple of Red-masked Parakeets (Psittacara erythrogenys) feeding on the fruit of a Bullet Tree (Terminalia buceras), just outside my window 

#MiamiWildlife 🪶

A lone Kentish Plover flying with Sanderling. 
Since 2007, the breeding population in Spain of the KP has dropped by 52% to an estimated 3,300 pairs. Heavy tourism, beach cleaning, extreme temperatures & dogs off leads are some causes. 🪶
This half-ounce blackpoll warbler undertakes an epic migration journey - from the boreal forest north of here to spend winter in South America. It didn't stay long in my garden 🪶

One of the easiest, best things we've ever done for your yard is just deciding to let most of it be. We had a slim dead tree that fell towards the back last year, and this log has been a focal point for all kinds of wildlife, including this male Pileated Woodpecker this morning. 🪶

Despite living in the so-called “Prairie State” I don’t have a lot of access to prairies 

Little Weaver Park in Urbana is a precious sliver of prairie, full of grassland sparrows and a rumored bobcat.

There’s a proposal to turn it into sports fields. Just utterly depressing.

Superb Lyrebird outside my window! There were actually two of them, but the other one was a little further back in the bushes. Here’s a 10-second video of it having a scratch. drive.google.com/file/d/1QIsc...
iconic Australian landscape - superb parrots at their nest hollow in an old red gum, with kangaroos grazing in the shade

Warning: R-rated content. Avert your eyes! 😄
Good to see the resident pied oystercatchers at Bald Hill Beach working on their family planning. They have been so unlucky. Every year they try. Every year they fail to become parents. Maybe this time?
🪶 #birds

The wiki entry for Say's phoebe says, "it prefers dry, desolate areas." So, like, a very popular beach. 😁

Natural Bridges SB, Santa Cruz, CA

Sunday afternoon went birding with a friend in South Louisiana. As the sun was setting we noticed a large amount of Scissor-tailed Flycatchers. A careful 1x1 count and got us 53! As we were leaving they started to roost in the Oak trees. Quite a sight just an hour south of New Orleans!
Warbler migration is ramping up. Fall warblers can be hard to identify, but thankfully black-and-white warblers are always recognizable. 🪶

Our Halloween-colored #BirdGlamour celebration continues with the Hooded Oriole! Seen in southwest US and Mexico, they’re also known as palm leaf orioles because the sew their sack nests on the undersides of palm fronds. Hooded Orioles are chatterboxes and will mimic woodpeckers and flycatchers!🧪🐡🐦

Tahiti petrel, wheeling over the Coral Sea. These are pelagic birds, rarely seen near land but abundant in certain parts of the Pacific Ocean.
#birds #ausbirds #ozwildlife
Spatiotemporal abundance of the whitespotted eagle ray and the giant manta ray (Chondrichthyes: Myliobatiformes) from aerial surveys in southeast Florida. 🦑🐟🌎🧪 link.springer.com/article/10.1...

Superb owl photo my wife took in 2016 in Brian Piccolo Park near Fort Lauderdale. The park is full of nesting burrowing owls who have no problem living among the people using the park for recreation, it’s really an amazing thing to see.

This palm warbler was one of a pair I saw zipping along near ground level, gleaning food. They eat primarily insects but will also eat seeds and berries. They also nest on the ground. Very un-warblery warbler! 🪶

🪶 A few white-faced storm-petrels have been seen at Altona this past fortnight. This migratory seabird has a global range and doesn't make its way into the bay very often. After three trips, I finally got this pic with about ten minutes of light left in the day.
Male hooded oriole. They are here in SoCal from about March through August for the most part & are usually found and heard around skirted palm trees.

Willow ptarmigan are such fun birds. Like many northern species, they moult into a nearly all-white plumage for winter to blend into their surroundings. However, they still stick out a bit when standing on crushed ice. 🪶🌿

A bar-tailed godwit developing its breeding plumage ahead of its big flight soon.
We don't often see this colouring in Australia. They fly way up north of the Arctic Circle to breed each year.
These birds hold the world record for non-stop flight.
Safe travels, buddy! 🤎
🪶 #birds

we get lots of raptors around our house, and although i generally don't care too much about bald eagles, i appreciate that they're a bird i can't mistake for anything else. there's no Davidson's Pale-Headed Megafalcon out there to trip me up when i'm trying to teach the kids what's flying overhead

Various Sandhill Crane head shots at the lakefront.

Male straight at ya, then three shots of the female. The last pic of the back of the head shows two gray feather bumps one on each side, those are the cranes ears.
A female Belted Kingfisher who was perched along the river this morning. I like how Kingfisher heads are big and their feet are small

Dramatic pose!

Ruby Throated Hummingbird migration still passing through the Florida Panhandle. Most of the males are long gone by now, but I'm still seeing the females, like this beautiful girl backlit by the morning sun.

The Bald Eagles at the local park will sometimes take sticks from the Great Blue Heron rookery and add them to their nest. This Bald Eagle picked up a stick, but the stick must not have met the Eagle's quality standards because it was dropped a few moments after this photo was taken.

We love this funky, punky Goldcrest, shared with our Nature Table group by Moi Hicks.
Weighing in at less than 6 grams, the Goldcrest is UK's smallest bird (along with the less common Firecrest).

It was unrelentingly grey, rainy and bitterly cold here all weekend but I was glad to have ventured out to Beddington Farmlands in south London when this handsome little Eurasian Kestrel landed in a tree about ten metres from me and decided to hang out. 🪶

#UrbanBirding #Birds #BritishBirds
You know you are on a cool bird when your local guide gets excited. Golden-hooded Tanager on Mountain Pine Ridge, Cayo, Belize, #birding

Cormorant comparison. 🪶 The big ones in front are pied cormorants, and the smaller ones are little pied cormorants. They're not often seen together as the little are more land based and the large ones are ocean-going.
Port of Brisbane, QLD #ausbirds

This is not the most exciting bird in the world, and it most likely will not make your day, but this is a Belding’s Savannah Sparrow, an endangered resident darling of the estuary, found only in salt marshes. 🪶🌿😎

Sungrebe (Heliornis fulica) at Caño Negro Wildlife Refuge.

Not really a grebe, but more closely related to rails.
American White Ibis working its way across a flooded field looking for lunch. I took this at Sheldon Lake state park.
Surfbirds at Alki Beach Christmas Eve. Surfbirds are small wading shore birds that nest on the Alaska tundra but overwinter on the Pacific Northwest coast: 🪶

The power of camouflage - black-faced sand grouse in Tarangire National Park, Tanzania. 

Protect habitat & biodiversity through working with local populations, ensuring they benefit from conservation.

Townsend's solitaire at a cemetery in Denver 🪶

It's the only "solitaire" native to the US, which are more closely related to bluebirds than other regional thrushes in Turdidae, like the robins or the Catharus "nightingale-thrushes".
🪶 Song sparrow eating a grasshopper and perching in a black-eyed Susan. Montrose Bird Sanctuary, Chicago, today. Could be wrong about bird and plant ID (but pretty sure about date and location!).

Great Blue Herons are typically solitary birds when out fishing. Very unusual to see two, let alone three or more, together, outside of a heron rookery. These three were part of a group of six herons fishing Alki Beach last summer: 🪶

Little Egret in Bishop's Stortford. Could be better, but nice to finally get a photo of my local patch bogey bird. Seen them on occasion but they are so skittish around here they usually take flight before I'm in range. Hopefully they get more used to the people and dog traffic along the Stort.

Spotted a reef heron at the beach twice now.  nzbirdsonline.org.nz/species/reef... "A bird of the rocky shore. It can also be seen on estuary mudflats and occasionally wading at sandy beaches. The population is estimated at only 300-500, but appears stable. Usually solitary."

When it's 15° F / -9.4° C out, an uneasy détente develops around the suet. The Downy Woodpecker, despite being the smallest woodpecker on the continent, is still an insane jackhammer dinosaur, and generally everyone keeps their distance. This Bluebird decided to roll the dice. Tense. 🪶 #bird

Celebrated the holiday by forcing my long-suffering father to search for hours for Red-cockaded Woodpeckers 

We found active-looking marked nest trees and ~6 million Brown-headed Nuthatches but no woodpeckers

Learned afterwards the park has wild flytraps, should’ve looked

Brown creeper photo would make a devilish jigsaw puzzle! These tiny birds stick close to the trunks of large loose-barked trees, probing for insects. The start at the bottom and work their way up, then move to the base of the next one 🪶

I’ve had few opportunities to get out this month but managed a quick visit to Rodley Nature Reserve in Leeds this morning. Grey and windy. 37 species, including goosander, grey wagtail, sparrowhawk, kingfisher. Enjoyable semi-urban #birding!

Planning your own "fall migration" for some leaf peeping? 🍂 Your journey probably won't take you as far as our Bird of the Week! Swainson's Hawks have the longest migration of any North American raptor. Learn about ABC's role in their recovery at bit.ly/3RvZURZ. 🪶

Question for Western Canadian-based birders 🪶 if there are any here: my wife and I spotted what we think was this hawk just outside Calgary, Alberta.  If it’s a ‘zone-tailed hawk’ it’s way out of its natural habitat. Is that possible, do you think? (img: @@286175)

Today started with very cold temperatures, I got to see some surprise visitors - Pine Siskins, whom I only see here every other odd year. They are goldfinch size, have pointy beaks, Goldfinch temperament and some yellow on the wings. Feisty little buggers.
#birds🪶 #Ostdrossel

A very nice birding day today!
I got a couple of lifers and finally managed an identifiable pic of the kingfisher that I've known lived around the river, but always evaded me.
Bull-headed Shrike モズ, Kingfisher カワセミ, and Gray Wagtail キセキレイ.
🪶#Birds #Japan

The threatened Tuturiwhatu (NZ dotterel) foraging on beach. also from the Friday hike.  These #birds are much harder to see than pīwakawaka, and present their own challenge to photograph. Beaches offer little cover for the photographer so I tend to shoot at maximum focal length.  📷🪶🇬🇸 
#sonyalpha

And these photos I took many years ago in November in Ternopil. It's not black&white photos. It's just that day was very grey and with dark monotonous sky. And countless ravens were sitting on the trees in the park. And then suddenly they started to fly simultaneously. Part 1.

Landing gear down!
Red-necked stints (like these at Bald Hill Beach) are our smallest migratory shorebirds.
They breed in Siberia and Alaska during our winter so rack up a lot of frequent flyer points.
🪶 #birds

This wedge-tailed eagle (different nest from the one I showed earlier) took me by surprise. I was wandering around Bundera Dam at Boolcoomatta looking for the crimson chats I had seen there the day before ... then looked up to see mama wedgie looking at me! This shows the scale of their nests. 🪶

We have a local barred owl pair that nests along the Salado Creek every year. While they were still in the area this year, they must’ve moved the location of the nest. They were bothered by a lot of curious folks last year and only one chick made it. I’m always happy to see them around though.

🧪🪶🌿

Dead chuffed the Bohemian Waxwings stuck around in Balham until I got back to London! It's turning out to be a great "waxwing winter" in the UK - so happy to have had the chance to see these gorgeous Scandinavian irruptive visitors feasting on berries just a 15 minute bike ride from home. 🪶
A Red-winged Blackbird fledgling sits in the grass, listening to Mom's constant contact calls - she is quite an acrobat as she hunts for bugs - a fledgling in the tree is fed 🪶🌿

My friend Barry, the Bare-throated Tiger-Heron at Tapir Valley Nature Reserve, Costa Rica. I was lucky to be able to spend many hours with this young bird as it learned to hunt along the edge of the canals. 

I would sit on the ground and it would hunt very close to me.

#birds #herons 🪶

Dad and Mom Mallard taking a relaxing swim while the little ones are safely resting on lily pads. Third in a series, that did not start out as a series. 🙂 My recollection is that either an Egret or Canada Goose was nearby and Dad was trying to scare them off. Lagoon, SF Bay.
#Birds 🪶
#Photography 📷

Went for a little wander around Leverett Pond yesterday and saw this slightly odd duck. Someone at iNaturalist tells me the bill coloration is probably the result of poor nutrition, not hybridization or anything like that? #birds 🪶

My favourite gull is the Great black-backed gull (not 'Greater black-back' as often named), which I'm pleased to say is often seen around the coasts here (southern England). It can reach 78 cm in length, weigh 2 kg, and have a wingspan of 1.65 m. The one here (from Weymouth) has a dirty throat.

#BirdGlamour celebrates the Altamira Oriole! This tropical oriole lives year-round in Mexico & Central America and they JUST makes it into southern TX/Rio Grande.  Females weave elaborate hanging nests up to 26 inches long. They love insects & forage dead grasshoppers off of the fronts of cars!🐡🧪🐦

White tern perched in an ironwood (Causuarina equisetifolia) tree, where the species "nests"... except they construct no nest to speak of! They precariously balance a bare egg within the ironwood's branches.

Photo from Saipan, CNMI 🪶

Yikes! A bald eagle is a rare sight in this area, but I caught one passing way overhead (P1). Zooming P1 shows it better, if blurry. But then he circled to fly directly over (P3), and as a zoom on P4 shows, looked right at me. 🦅 📸📷

Last week I got to watch a particularly striking Song Sparrow foraging in the fallen leaves and berries of a California Pepper Tree - I managed to get a shot just before it chomped down on this rolled up isopod 🪶

Low pass from an immature white-bellied sea eagle 🪶 Adults are a bit larger, and all white on their front and head.
Port of Brisbane, QLD
#ausbirds

There are also white-throated dippers on the Pader.  The Pader is the shortest river in Germany.  Up to 5000 liters per second flow from around 200 springs and the first mill is operated after 400 m. #birds #scanjack #paderborn

Open water on the lakes and ponds around Anchorage #Alaska is getting pretty scarce but Common Goldeneyes can get by with surprisingly little water. A few usually manage to stick it out after most other ducks have bailed on us. 🪶

Black Phoebe. These birds have a long range - from the western US south all the way to Argentina. They are primarily insectivorous. If you love birds, gotta conserve bugs. 🌿🧪🪶
Tatama National Park, Colombia

Oystercatcher searching for food in rocks exposed during low tide along shoreline of San Francisco Bay in Emeryville, California.  Early sundown for first photo and sun has almost set in second. (I saw an Oystercatcher years ago and it took another year or so to see another.)
#Birds 🦉
#Photography 📷

I was invited to speak at the Colombia Birdfair in Cali, Colombia last week. The birding was amazing, I was able to see and take pics of amazing birds, this is just the tip of the iceberg!
Cali is hosting the COP16 so if you wanna come visit this would be a perfect time!

Green Honeycreeper (male) 🪶

I think Waterthrush Wednesday sounds like a great idea! Waterthrushes are some of my favorite spring warblers, even though they can be a bit elusive. Here's a Northern Waterthrush from last spring, showing off its insect hunting skills. 🪶🌿
This yellow billed magpie wants you to slow down.  Endemic CA bird with very limited range in central CA.  Vineyard Drive Paso Robles wine 🍷 country. 🌿🪶
Phonescoped distant Little Gull at Eoligarry on a blustery Saturday. A Jackdaw, a species we only get one or two of each year on Barra, in a good productive field for crows this year 😂 #UKBirding

Nine cockatoos turned up to eat berries so I went out to get a pic. Did they fly away? No they did not. Four of them flew over to the rail near me, stared a bit and started preening. Or snogging. Very shy.
This CA Scrub Jay at our hotel comes over to everyone’s patio decks begging for food. He has been eating out of our hands -sunflower ￼seeds and peanuts all week.  So tame and bold. 🪶🌿

Bird Glamour’s Glamager series continues with the striking Scarlet Tanager! This eastern North America & South American tanager are surprisingly difficult to spot: they prefer to hang out high in the forest canopy. They also do a wardrobe change in the fall until spring to a yellow-green plumage! 🐡

what a Great Egret ! great egrets differ from snowy egrets in that they are a bit larger, with a longer neck, darker legs, and have a yellowish bill! Snowy egrets get longer feathers on their head while great egrets get them on their rump ^v^

never got around to posting this little asshole. they'd fly up high, drop the crustacean, and then fly down and try again. except it wasn't just the floor. the car they're perched on also had the thing dropped from a great height, making a mighty metallic clunking sound. #birds 🪶
The monk parakeets of Avenue L. I saw maybe two dozen of them near the Chicago Skyway. A neighborhood resident, who was standing in his garage, said, "They make a lot of noise."

i saw bald eagles today. on january first, in canada. they made their gay little chitters (no hawk-like shrieks as USian propaganda would have you believe), sitting on trees, contending with one another, flapping their mighty wings. they are beautiful animals.

Blue-winged Mountain Tanagers are common, widespread tanagers found in the Andean cloud forest. Their favorites food are insects, fruits, and small berries. And yes, this bird is a stunner. It is unmistakable with its bright yellow, black, and electric blue coloring. 🪶

I've been fascinated with 🪶 Boat-billed Herons as long as I've known they existed, it was a privilege to observe this mating pair discussing suitable nesting sites this morning in Tortuguero National Park, Costa Rica

First snake eagle of the year just seen banking over the hills beyond the motorway outside of Ioannina. The fingers on its massive wings riffling with wind; its pale feathers ghostly against the slanted sunlight. Travel well, beautiful bird.

You might have seen my "Manny" the Mandarin stuff...

From that time we took our grandson to see bald eagles and feed the ducks out at Lynx Lake...and he called me over to 'check out the two weird ducks" he found....🤯

TWO Mandarin drakes...in Arizona. 🪶🌿

Manny wasn't shy, so he got all the love!

A fantastic photo to start your week. Taken by my friend Alan McFadyen, who runs local wildlife photography hides. He took the famous pic of a kingfisher diving vertically, just before it entered the water.

There are some species at that are on the top 10 most wanted birds of many searchers and the Diademed sandpiper-plover is one of those. What a thrill when I finally caught up with this species high in the Andes of #Peru a decade ago. 🪶

This past year was wild! We had storms that brought some mountain bluebirds to nearly sea level for about 3 weeks in the spring.

For context, the park I frequent is 2 miles from the Pacific Ocean. The bluff overlooking the park where this bird was found is no higher than 100 feet in elevation! 🪶

A Great Blue Heron from the Anchorage Coastal Wildlife Refuge #Alaska (last month). An everyday bird for many of you, well those in North America, but we are at the very edge of the range and lucky to get a sighting a year.

Steller’s sea eagle Vlad probably sees you before you see him at Zoo Boise. Eagle eyes can see four to five times further than a person with perfect vision! 👁️

📷: ZooTeen Link

What a day! Visited Last Mountain Lake Migratory Bird Sanctuary (Saskatchewan, Canada) to spend a day watching the greatest show on earth. Saw about 30 bird species, and easily 50,000 individuals. Geese, ducks, cranes, harriers, eagles... 🪶

It has a pinkish-brown body, striking black and white wings, a black downcurved bill, and a pinkish-brown crest which it raises when excited.
   The Eurasian hoopoe is native to Europe, Asia and the northern half of Africa.

For many of you a common bird but White-throated Sparrows are quite rare in #Alaska. This one has been spending the winter in Anchorage. Same spot as there was one last winter but I didn't get a picture to compare. 🪶

Birdfeeder Drama:

Last week our lone blue-jay learned that mimicking a hawk will cause every squirrel at the feeder to dive for cover.

Just now he tried it on the doves and is visibly perplexed that it didn't fucking work, so he flew down close and tried again.

Then the doves beat his ass.

I got to see this very photogenic lifer yesterday: a brilliant white Lark Sparrow that was hanging out in a flock of finches and bluebirds, foraging among the dew-covered grass at a local campground. 🪶

The terror of Montezuma Rainforest Lodge. This little Rufous-tailed Hummingbird liked to hide in the bushes an attempt to chase off any other hummingbird in his territory.
Tatamà National Park, Colombia 🧪🌿🪶

Just heard a Carolina Wren make a noise I’ve never heard a Carolina wren make before, kind of a soft trill, almost like a gray tree frog.  If I hadn’t been staring at the bird I would have been utterly baffled. 🪶

A drake Common Goldeneye stretching his wings at @@299642 Saltholme reserve yesterday. Small numbers of them are wintering on the East Saltholme Pool and usually a few may linger until second half of April. #ukbirding #ukwildlife 🪶

Outside of Kazakhstan: Finally we could watch the migration of the Eurasian crane in Kelbra, Germany. It was fantastic to see thousands of birds arriving in dusk at this lake to fly later further to Spain. These birds also living in Kazakhstan and migrating in winter to India.
It’s not every Monday morning I’m woken by four surprisingly large water buffalo drinking and relieving themselves in the pond by my shepherd’s hut. (Coombeshead Rewilding Centre, Devon).

Black-crowned Night-heron is one of the few birds we've seen in Taiwan that *isn't* a lifer. But unlike home, they are amazingly abundant here. Herons, egrets, night-herons, bitterns, all super common and easy to admire. #birding
Not much gets wasted in nature. A piece of snakeskin is good nesting material for this Glossy Starling.

🧪One of the coolest critters I’ve ever seen, an aptly named rhinoceros katydid (Copiphora rhinoceros) spotted in the Monteverde area of Costa Rica. I’ve never been much of an entomology gal, but this guy sold me 🦗

Here's a great blue heron (I believe?) taking off from a pond in suburban Kansas City. The kids around the pond were in awe of the it flying away! Good reminder that urban wildlife is so important for connecting kids with their environment. 

🦉

Greater Yellowlegs and its little buddy Lesser Yellowlegs giving a nice rare comparison this evening at Steigerwald Lake in  Washougal, Washington. Greater is not interested in sharing its salamander. #birding

A rainbow lorikeet about to stick its head in a tree. 🪶

Absolutely stunning bird; lovely call.  It always astounds me how something like this can so completely disappear after landing in a tree.

Canon R6mkii + RF100-400mm

Yesterday while out with the chickens as they free ranged, we saw the world's tiniest hawk regarding us from a tree branch above—presumably a male sharp-shinned hawk. He was about the size of a pigeon.

'Death glares' from giant petrels, squadrons of great cormorants, fly-bys from fulmars, A-list of albatross: never a shortage of seabirds in the Southern Ocean spotted on CSIRO #RVInvestigator 
🪶 aappartnership.org.au/soaring-on-s...

Barnegat part 11B: Harlequin Ducks edition continued. At Barnegat, these ducks like to hang out on the inlet side of the jetty, fairly close to the jetty walls. When they’ve shown up locally, they tend to hang out by our breakwalls along the beach, where Lake Erie tends to be the roughest. 🪶

A great blue heron just landed on my head while we’re sailing. I… don’t understand. I wouldn’t have believed it if my friend hadn’t seen the whole thing. It jumped off since I was obviously very startled.

White-throated Mountaingem, first two photos are the male, last one is female, both stunning!

White-throated Mountain-gem
(Lampornis castaneoventris)
November 25, 2023
Savegre Valley - Miriam’s

#birds #hummingbirds

I also heard weird noises coming from my chimney. It sounded like the crunching of bones.
I thought it was migratory birds at first, so two years ago I set up a sound-activated camera looking up the chimney to capture the source. It took this photo.

I spent a small amount of time watching pacific ladder-backed — I mean… Nuttall’s woodpeckers… mating

After he finishes, he stays a while and strokes the back of her head a few times before they leave 

Such an intimate behavior to witness 🥰🪶

Still going thru my Alaska pics-- here;s a nice Red Phalarope Female in her bright breeding plumage. Utqiagvik (Barrow) Alaska, the Northhern-most city in North America is a great place to see shorebirds up close. #Birds

耕運機が真横を通り過ぎてもまったく動じないノスリw 耕運機で田んぼを耕すと、その土に一斉に野鳥が群がります。そこを狙っているんですよね～。普通空からが一般的な気もしますがちょっと楽してるノスリです💦

A buzzard that does not flee when a cultivator approaches, but aims at its prey. Normally, I would be inclined to aim from the sky, but this buzzard is trying to aim from the ground with ease.
A vagrant Hepatic Tanager in Stockton Springs marks my 350th species of bird observed in Maine. A lovely bird to end the year on.

My God is the Sun

Turkey vulture sunning itself atop the electrical pole at the end of my sister’s driveway. Lakeside, CA, 2023

Wildlife in my local #OnePlaceStudy. Fifteen species (butterflies, birds, wildflowers, dragonflies, grey squirrel, a fungus) caught on camera over the last couple of years at Fawsley in Northamptonshire, where I study the dead and photograph the living!

princess parrots - one of Australia's least known birds, these nomads live in the driest deserts and every so often turn up in places where people live. 
This pic is from when i got lucky enough to see them in 2009 🪶

In good light, the range of colours on rainbow lorikeets 🪶 seems to be infinite. They really wear the whole visible spectrum. #ausbirds
Dowse Lagoon, Brisbane QLD

This kestrel was hovering low on a breezy day at Cuckmere. It was amazing to see how it could keep its head so perfectly still whilst being buffeted by the wind.

#birds #ukwildlife

Barnegat part 10…A😅: Long-tailed Ducks. One of two duck species this spot is particularly famous for. There were several huge rafts feeding well beyond the inlet of hundreds of ducks, but plenty up close, too. Took tons of pics, so first up are the males in their fancy winter colors. 🪶

Cedar Waxwing
 
They are elegant-looking birds named for beads of shiny red material on the tips of the secondary wing feathers.
      It breeds in northern forests of Eurasia and America and every few years irrupts far southward in winter.

If you love birds, you need to conserve bugs. In this BugShot - Birding crossover event we have a Lemon-browed Flycatcher with a katydid. The bird say there with it so it was probably waiting for us to leave so it could head to its nest. 🌿🪶#inverts

Bird of the day is the Bohemian Waxwing. I only have crap photos that don’t do these beautiful birds justice. Rare winter visitors to the UK, it’s going to be a good year for spotting them. More than 1000 already in Scotland and northern England….but they’re moving south. (Alt for more) 🪶
A mallard drake's plumage catches the light four different ways. They're so common that we forget about how fabulous they are somtimes #birds
Winter has come to Portland and the birds are doing their best to keep warm. This Hermit Thrush wears a puffed mantle of feathers while visiting Our Lady of Guadalupe. #birding

Spotted this handsome Limpkin at City Park in New Orleans. In Nashville, we have a lone Limpkin at Centennial Park that caused quite a stir, being uncommon to the area. He's still hanging out there despite the fact that the cold has set in. The snails must be delectable. #birding #birdwatching

A different side to galahs. 🪶

Female on the right with red eye; male on the left with black eye. They mate for life, and these two were wonderfully affectionate.

Oh I just saw a Sanderling last week! This one was wading in the mud, not running up and down on a beach, because migrating shorebirds have to make do with what's available. (Sanderling, Ohio, October 2023) 🪶
#birds here is a yellow rumped  warbler on a red maple, these guys are all over Southeast Louisiana right now munching on wax myrtle berries, which is why they used to be called myrtle warblers.

Sometimes an enormous bird flies over so close you have to zoom out to fit it in the frame. This was at only 300mm on my zoom lens. 
White-bellied sea eagle 🪶
Brisbane QLD

Here's an interesting bird, hiding amongst the red-necked stints.
It's a double-banded plover which migrates from New Zealand.
We don't see many of these at Bald Hill Beach each year.
🪶 #birds

My only somewhat-successful long-exposure shot so far of one of the visiting long-eared owls in Central Park, hunting after flyout earlier this week. They are small and fast, and very hard to track at night! 🦉🌃

(5 second exposure, using only ambient light, taken from a respectful distance)

I pulled back to get a wider shot to show you the two wedge-tailed eagles at Boolcoomatta. Can you see them? One on the right and one in the distance on the left. These are the parents of that baby wedgie I showed you. Not sure, but I think the one on the right is the male.
🪶

The full set of kingfishers spotted on our Darwin trip: azure, blue-winged kookaburra, forest and sacred (we possibly saw a little kingfisher on the wetland cruise, but it zipped by too fast to be sure). #birds
Common Redshank takes off from the shallow lake. You can tell it's a Common by the white colour on the trailing edges of the wings. 🪶
Some floofy cuteness from my time in the taiga last week. Canada jays (whisky jacks) are truly cold-weather birds. They have thick plumage to keep them warm and start nesting in February! 🌿🦉

…so soft and gentle; they sing to no one save themselves. Not loud & boastful like the mocker; not full of chatter like the purple martin. The bluebird song is a kind & personal “I love you” that one must be close & quiet to hear. 
~Kenny Kleinpeter

💙📷 Western Bluebird male, aka Sialia mexicana 🪶🌿

Stormfly is not impressed with the snow, or with being asked to spend some time outside today. Though most migrate, some American kestrels actually stay in Minnesota all winter, which is a pretty crazy feat for such a tiny falcon!

🦉🪶 Once, at a guided group tour of a local nature reserve, I spotted a great egret, which we call a "great silver heron", and the boomer woman beside me confidently corrected me: "no, he's white, not silver, I can see it quite clearly." I couldn't argue with that.  (grote zilverreiger, Ardea alba)

Semi-stormy morning/lunch gave stunning views of a young Pomarine Skua hunting at the beach. Surprisingly, when all was calm(ish), ≥25 Eur + 1 Leach's #Storm-petrels were still around—in beautiful evening sunlight! Some new local #birds and "my" #eBird hotspot's broken 100 species & turned green. 😊
Finally made the effort to add #blackgrouse to my Yorks list. These males were photographed from the car. A total of 17 seen in Arkengarthdale. Stunning, even in the drizzle! #ukbirding

Mr. Blue Grosbeak seen at Sevilleta National Wildlife Refuge in New Mexico last summer. Rarely do birds perch so obligingly but this guy remained perched on the mesquite for about a dozen shots! 🪶#birds

A rose-ringed parakeet and a black drongo in a conflict over perching rights.

The hummingbirds have gotten so chonky to prep for migration that the tone of their buzzing has dropped, a sure sign that fall is here.
if we're posting woodpeckers, here's the first one i found: nuttall's woodpecker, drumming away right at eye level thanks to picking a tree at the bottom of a slope 🪶

More superhero sea ducks! Here's a female Common Eider. She's floating in calm seas now but can dive and float in spectacular waves like it's no big deal.

An Anna's Hummingbird's gorget changes color with the slightest change in angle of light. Is it orange or magenta? #Birding the backyard

With the slightly warmer temperatures and finally some sun, a lot of the redpolls that were here during the blizzard, have gone looking for food elsewhere. There are still a few around though, and this male common redpoll arrived just before sunset to forage in the trees!🪶
Belize's Black Pheobes are black lower on their front than I am used to seeing in Oregon and California-- and at least two I've seen have small white splotches on their heads. #birding

Here's a female Great-tailed Grackle. A "garbage" bird of San Antonio, but I love them. They are loud, flamboyant, and gather in great numbers all over town. This one was enjoying a fountain at the Pearl in downtown. 🪶

Dry bread makes your cheeks red, goes a German saying. However, cold air has the same effect. I look similar to this Redbellied Woodpecker after going outside to refill feeders and baths. Stay warm, everybody! 🪶

My favourite corvids: blue jays! Brilliant mimics, long memories, highly intelligent, curious, friendly, and full of opinions - I love my little flock who come up to the window to ask for peanuts every day.

#Birding with mom today at Bonneville Fish Hatchery. We didn't see too many species, but the young 1st-molt Brewer's Blackbirds were out in great numbers. They are getting their first adult plumage through the grayer fledgling coat.

🪶🦉Time for some woodpeckers! The most common species around here (North-Belgium) is clearly the great spotted woodpecker. Not very shy, comes for the peanuts just outside my kitchen window. And usually announces itself loud and clearly. (grote bonte specht, Dendrocopos major)

Saw a lot of golden-crowned kinglets in Redwood Regional this morning. Gorgeous, frenetic birds. The bit where the Stream and Tres Sendas trails meet is fast becoming one of my fav birding spots in the east bay.

A first look at a Sandhill Crane this year at the Lakefront. It will take a while to tell if these are the same mated pair from last year. Looks pretty good after touching down from traveling 100s of miles. Only stayed briefly with them letting them acclimate in their urban home.🪶
Our li'l Green Woodpecker is back digging a nice hole in the lawn. He (or she) makes me unfeasibly happy for being a small green bird with a red splodge on their head.
Little Spiderhunter (Arachnothera longirostra) As I was photographing a banana tree at Bondla, Goa this active little bird dropped in nicely for a few pics #birds #goa #india

He's probably not pondering trying to catch that big ol' crane fly(?) in the upper right of the photo, but hummingbirds do eat insects. If you love birds, gotta help conserve bugs. 🌿🧪🪶
Purple-throated Woodstar 
Tatama National Park, Colombia

📷 Once known as the Cactus Woodpecker, the Ladder-backed Woodpeckers (Dryobates scalaris) occasionally hybridize with their closest living relative, Nuttall’s Woodpecker. 🪶🌿

I bring it up because the last bird I reposted is a Nuttall's...AND it's Woodpecker Wednesday! 🤘🤓

Common Bronzewing in my Merimbula backyard this evening. 
A wary pigeon, widely distributed in Australia.

The Red-breasted sapsuckers are back. They aren't great for our trees, but I love them. This guy did not want his picture taken. It was raining and kept going to the opposite side of the tree to avoid me and my camera, but I finally got the little bugger. 🪶

A Fawn-throated Foliage-gleaner (Automolus cervinigularis) working on its nest along one of the forest trails at Tapir Valley.

A pair of cedar waxwings, with one enjoying some nice berries. Algonkian Regional Park, Sterling, Virginia, USA 8DEC2023. #birds

What a delight it's been to have 4 Waxwings at Cosmeston for the past week or so. Rather than flycatching above distant trees, on Thursday they were eating hips in a location where I could use a hedge as a hide. They are such stunning birds!

Rose-breasted grosbeaks are stunning birds that prefer woodlands and forest edges, so we don’t see them often in the city. This male was foraging deep in the branches but eventually moved to a small gap for a better look!🪶

My beloved Barrow's Goldeneyes will be heading back up to Alaska soon, so it was very good to get in the water with this them afternoon. The waves were up today, and I was working on capturing a sense of the ever-shifting, endlessly-dynamic environment that these wonderful birds inhabit. 🪶

Birds at Coton nature reserve near #Cambridge today (the ones that sat still long enough to photo):
Redwing, Pied Wagtail,
Crow, Buzzard.
Fieldfares more numerous but much too flighty!

A male yellow-rumped warbler posed for me in an aspen tree this spring. This is one of the most common warblers in North America, and in my opinion also one of the prettiest

🦉🌱 A question for birders out there. Are starlings the only birds who murmurate? Just had a large flock of small birds swooped above me in the garden with a distinct sound then off they went towards the blackberry brambles, then on to a tall fir tree in a distance. Thanks!

Bar-tailed godwits hold the world record for long distance travel. One tracked bird travelled 13,560km over 11 days non-stop! I only saw two godwits at Bald Hill Beach on the weekend.
IUCN Red List: Near threatened
🪶 #birds
The wintering Pallid Harrier at Warham Greens pulled some amazing shapes, even turning upside down, to evade the mobbing Crows on its way to roost on Saturday evening. #birds

A Strong-billed Woodcreeper skulking about the Andean cloud forest. It's always fun catching a bird like this through dense foliage. 🌿🧪🪶
Bellavista Cloud Forest Reserve, Ecuador

Now pregaming with an actual superb (and sleepy) owl 🪶🌿🦉🧪⚒️
I have only seen these burrowing owls at the Salton Sea, where I took students on a San Andreas Fault field trip (faults, birds, mud volcanoes and obsidian all in one spot!)
White-tailed Ptarmigan on the hunt for buds. Compared to the other ptarmigan, white-tails seem to operate in slow motion, at least during the winter. Chugach State Park #Alaska 🪶

There's still time to catch the stunning winter wildlife spectacle of a Starling murmurations.

Barry Cook took these photos in Brighton last week. Eastbourne seafront is also a good place to witness murmurations.
Not an every day sight for me. A Solitary Sandpiper foraging. Sadly, even deep in the field I still find plastic bottles. 🦉📷🌿

From 2020 in my gallery and one of Nature's many wonders, a jaw-dropping murmuration of hundreds of starlings. Fortunately, this took place while travelling along a country road and not a major highway where I've also witnessed this spectacular formation. 🪶

I've seen Olive-backed Orioles in our area before, but this is the first time I've seen one in our garden! It found a big, juicy (and hairy) caterpillar.

Not all ladybird beetles are red with black spots! Scroll through this gallery to see yellow, pink, grey, black, and even nondescript furry brown.

🪶🦉I mean, why did they call all of our birds here the "common this" and the "common that"? This wonder of nature shouldn't be called a "common" crane. I always enjoy seeing and photograph this bird🪶,the beautiful Red-ruffed fruitcrow, you can find him in Colombia, Venezuela, Ecuador, Peru, south eastern Brazil and north eastern Argentina. I saw him the first time in my house and got enchanted with him!🤩 Have you heard of him? 
#birds #photography

Anyone else love horseflies? Tabanus lineola or the striped horsefly is incredibly beautiful. Of course I am even more in love with this sweetie, the green eyed horsefly. I know most people don’t like them, but still they are beautiful.

Let's pretend it's July 23rd instead of January 23rd. Please enjoy this Black Skimmer skimming! They zoom along the surface of the water and catch fish and look so elegant doing it. 

#birds 🌿 #marinelife

While I was filming Mamita (the matriarch Baird's Tapir at Tapir Valley, Costa Rica) last night, my friend Donald was surreptitiously filming me.

The tapirs here are getting very relaxed with people, as poaching has been dramatically reduced and they're starting to see us as safe.

The Bay Wren (Cantorchilus nigricapillus) is a common (and very loud) species of the Caribbean slope of Costa Rica.

The Gold-ringed Tanager has the local common name Bangsia de Tatama for the very limited range where it is found. The tanager is the symbol of Tatama National Park and a species that has inspired conservation in Colombia. 🪶🌿

The Common Kingfisher has to be one of the UK's most beautiful birds: it looks like it belongs in the tropics rather than fishing along a stream in a wee London park next to a children's playground, but that's where I found this handsome chap this morning in Colliers Wood. 🪶

Hundreds of canvasback ducks in the Niagara River yesterday. Was able to count four separate paddlings but nowhere to pull over along the Niagara Parkway for closer pics. Here are two of the four mass gatherings we came across, always an amazing sight to see even on a very dull (& difficult) day. 🪶

The great white pelican also known as the eastern white pelican, rosy pelican or simply white pelican is a bird in the pelican family. 
            It breeds from southeastern Europe through Asia and Africa, in swamps and shallow lakes.

Black Phoebes used to be rare to infrequent in Oregon, especially the Northern parts, but now are more and more common. This one graced our presence at Eagle Lookout at Ankeny National Wildlife Refuge today in Marion County, Oregon. #birding

I was delighted to find my wife her lifer Clapper Rail two weeks ago (at Huntington Beach State Park in South Carolina, USA)

Gotta admit, though, this individual is pretty rumbled-looking, rough lifer for a species that is often sleek!

Townsend's warblers (Setophaga townsendi) are a migratory visitor to the Gorge in winter. They like to forage & breed high in the treetops of conifers; large-diameter trees are preferred for breeding. For that reason, you won't find them in most urban areas. My yard has ...
On a grey wet day in London, a black-headed gull perches by the River Thames, with St Paul’s Cathedral in the background.  The gull is re-growing its breeding plumage 🪶

Mt Laguna is also home to one of my all-time favorite birds: the Acorn Woodpecker! This time they were busy gorging themselves on the abundant insects flying over Little Laguna Lake, unseasonably filled by the tropical storm 🪶
A sandhill crane making its way to an evening roosting location in San Luis valley, Colorado. #birding 🪶📷 #nature
Check out these beauties. Often overlooked, stock doves are the classy, understated cousins of the clumsy, oafish wood pigeon. 🪶

The pyrrhuloxia or desert cardinal is a bird that inhabits the arid region from the southwestern United States to central Mexico.
It is similar to the northern cardinal in terms of song and behavior, and both coexist in many desert areas.

The Schleich 2022 caracal. Caracals aren't lynxes nor close to them, but part of the same lineage as African golden cats and servals. Famous for their ability to leap vertically and grab birds in flight, and for their long-tufted, communicative ears.
